parsed.anchor_rejected_above:
Level 4 fails on multiple required anchors. (a) The reservation countdown is computed once per render with no setInterval/state tick — `BookDetailPage.tsx:10-22 formatTimeRemaining` is called inline at line 103 inside JSX with no useEffect to re-render, so the HH:MM:SS does NOT 'update without page reload'. (b) The catalog has no secondary affordance: `BooksPage.tsx:42-51` renders `
{books.map(book => )}
` with zero sort, filter, or search controls. (c) Confirm-destructive actions lack a distinct danger treatment — `MemberDetailPage.tsx:130-139` uses default-primary `` (a `.btn-danger` class exists in `index.css:200-207` but is unused in any flow). (d) Visual emphasis is mostly color, not 'color + icon': the overdue badge `index.css:484-491` and `MemberDetailPage.tsx:38 {loan.days_overdue} days overdue` is text+color only with no icon.

// GET /api/members/:id/loans/history - Get member's loan history
  router.get('/:id/loans/history', (req, res) => {
parsed.anchor_rejected_above: Anchor 4 fails on three counts: (1) error responses contain only a human `error` string with no stable machine-readable `code` field — domain errors map to HTTP via class checks per handler but the wire payload exposes no code (e.g., no 'LOAN_LIMIT_REACHED'); (2) the OpenAPI spec contains zero `examples` for the negative ACs (402, 403, 409 responses each just `$ref` the Error schema with no example body); (3) response envelopes are not fully consistent — `POST /reservations/:id/cancel` returns `{success: true}` while sibling mutations like `POST /loans/:id/return` return the full resource. Additionally, `GET /api/members/:id/loans/history` is implemented in routes/members.ts but is absent from openapi.yaml, a minor implementation/spec drift that prevents the 'every endpoint present' clause of anchor 3 from being clean and certainly blocks anchor 4.

parsed.anchor_rejected_above: Anchor 4 (Well-modularized) does not match because: (a) services call raw `db.prepare(...)` directly throughout loanService.ts (e.g., `const memberStmt = db.prepare('SELECT status FROM members WHERE id = ?');`) rather than going through a thin DB-access abstraction; (b) the frontend is organized by technical role (`components/`, `hooks/`, `pages/`, `types/`) — not by feature (no `checkout/`, `members/`, `books/` feature folders); (c) shared types are duplicated — frontend/src/types/index.ts redeclares `Loan`, `LoanWithBook`, `Book`, `Member` etc. that already exist in the backend services (e.g., `loanService.ts` exports its own `Loan` and `LoanWithBook`), with no shared workspace or generated client.

completeness: Running `npm run test:bdd` fails to load step definitions: `SyntaxError: The requested module '@playwright/test' does not provide an export named 'APIRequestContext' at ModuleJobSync.runSync` — and steps.ts contains zero step bindings for lending/returns/reservations/fines scenarios (e.g., no Given for `a member "Alice" with ID "m1" is active`, no When for `the librarian lends the book to the member`, no Then for `a fine of $3.00 is created`). Per rubric note, this caps completeness at 2.
alignment: @AC-3.2\n  Scenario: Reject loan when member has reached loan limit
@AC-4.2\n  Scenario: Return a book very late with fine cap

Every scenario across all 6 feature files carries exactly one @AC-x.y tag, and scenario titles are human-readable restatements of the AC. However, AC-1.3, AC-3.6, AC-4.5, AC-5.6, and AC-6.4 have no scenarios — bidirectional traceability fails.
declarative: Good: `When the librarian lends the book to the member` / `Then the loan is created` / `And sets the due date to 14 days from now` (lending.feature:13-15)

Bad (HTTP status leakage throughout): `Then the system returns a 409 error` (lending.feature:22), `Then the system returns a 402 error` (lending.feature:36), `Then the system returns a 404 error` (catalog.feature:25) — implementation-leaky terms in business specs prevent score 4.
atomic: Given a member "Alice" with ID "m1" is active
And a book "The Hobbit" with ID "b1" has available copies
When the librarian lends the book to the member
Then the system creates a loan
And sets the due date to 14 days from now
And decreases the available copies by 1
Slight compound exception: `And each book shows title, author, ISBN, total copies, and available copies` (catalog.feature:13)
